title: Postman
description: Essential Postman CLI commands and patterns for API testing and automation.

commands.description: Run a Postman collection using Newman.
commands.scenario: Use when you want to execute a Postman collection from the command line.

commands.description: Run a collection with a specific environment.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to test against different environments.

commands.description: Run a collection and output results in CLI and JSON format.
commands.scenario: Use when you need detailed test reports in multiple formats.

commands.description: Set a request timeout for a collection run.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to limit the time a request can take.

commands.description: Add a delay between requests in a collection run.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to throttle requests to the server.

commands.description: Stop the collection run on the first failure.
commands.scenario: Use when you want to halt execution on errors.

commands.description: Run a specific folder within a collection.
commands.scenario: Use when you want to test a subset of requests.

commands.description: Set a global variable for the collection run.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to override global variables temporarily.

commands.description: Export the environment variables after a run.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to save the state of environment variables.

commands.description: Export global variables after a run.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to save global variables state.

commands.description: Prevent Newman from following HTTP 3xx responses.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to test redirect behavior.

commands.description: Disable SSL verification for the run.
commands.scenario: Use when testing against servers with self-signed certificates.
Use when you need plain text output for logs.
